original: Red Sox rookie Bob Ojeda no-hits Yanks for 8 innings before Rick Cerone &Dave Winfieldlead off 9th with back-to-back doubles
Indonesia is hit with an earthquake that measures 8.1 on the Richter scale
Mae Jemisonis the 1st African American woman to go into space (aboard Endeavour STS-47)
Four teens follow their dog down a hole near Lascaux, France, and discover 17,000-year-old drawings now known as the Lascaux Cave Paintings
Albert Rizzo trod water at sea for 108 hours 9 minutes
Gilbert, strongest hurricane ever (160 mph), devastates Jamaica
Wang Junxia runs a women's 3000 m world record (8:12.29) at the Chinese National Games
Hong Kong Disneyland opens in Penny's Bay on Lantau Island, Hong Kong
Jack Kilbydemonstrates his first integrated circuit (IC) to his supervisor
Conrad Shinn, American pilot and the first person to land a plane at the South Pole, born in Leaksville, North Carolina (d. 2025)[1]
Caroline Aigle, French first woman fighter pilot in the French Air Force aged 25, born in Montauban, France (d. 2007)
Highest football score recorded in any 1st-class soccer match: Arbroath 36–0 Bon Accord in Scotland
Annie Londonderry [Annie Kopchovsky] arrives in Chicago to complete first round-the-world trip by a woman on a bicycle in 15 months and collects her $10,000 prize
Chicago White Sox Minnie Miñoso (52) singles off California Angles Sid Monge in the second inning of a 2–1, 10-inning win, for his first MLB hit after twelve years of retirement
NASA launches STS-47 (Endeavour 2)
Hans Zimmer, German Grammy and Academy Award-winning film score composer (The Lion King; Dune (2021); Inception; Kung Fu Panda), born in Frankfurt, West Germany
Luna 2 is launched by the Soviet Union, the first spacecraft to impact the Moon
Film that started Spaghetti Western genre "A Fistful of Dollars" premieres in Italy, directed bySergio Leone, and starringClint Eastwoodin his first leading role
NASA's Gemini XI with Pete Conrad and Richard F. Gordon aboard, launched for 71-hour flight
Space shuttle STS 48 (Discovery 14) launched
French astronomerCharles Messiermistakenly identifies the Crab Nebula and begins his Messier Catalogue
Future Baseball Hall of Fame pitchers dual for final time; Boston Rustlers'Cy YoungvsChristy Mathewsonof the Giants; NY wins, 11-2
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 34,840 m
US Supreme Court orders the all-white Central High School in Little Rock, Arkansas, to integrate
Animated cartoon series "The Smurfs" byHanna-Barberais first broadcast in North America
RM [Kim Nam-joon], South Korean rapper (BTS), born in Ilsan, South Korea
Hurricane Inuki pounds Hawaii
Traditional date of theBattle of Marathon, where a small Athenian force defeats the Persian Empire
John Flamsteedsees partial solar eclipse, stirs his interest in astronomy

423 die when steamship SS Central America, the 'Ship of Gold', sinks in a hurricane off Cape Romain, South Carolina, carrying tonnes of gold coins and bricks from the California Gold Rush (rediscovered 1988)
WUSF TV channel 16 in Tampa-St Petersburg, FL (PBS) 1st broadcast
STS-51 (Discovery) launches into orbit
Lawrence Brooks, American US Army soldier, 1940-45 (91st Engineer Battalion - New Guinea, The Philippines), and longest-living American World War II veteran, born in Norwood, Louisiana (d. 2022)
Stanislaw Lem, Polish sci-fi author (Solaris, Invincible), born in Lwów, Second Polish Republic (d. 2006)
Neil Peart, Canadian rock drummer and lyricist (Rush - "Limelight"; "Peaceable Kingdom"), born in Hamilton, Ontario (d. 2020)
Chip Beck, American golfer (US Open 1986, 89, Masters 1993 runner-up; shot 59 in 1991), born in Fayetteville, North Carolina
Leó Szilárd, waiting for a red light on Southampton Row in Bloomsbury, conceives the idea of a nuclear chain reaction

San Felipe Segundo/Okeechobee hurricane makes landfall in Guadeloupe, French Antilles as category 4 storm, killing 1,200; additional damage and fatalities on neighboring islands of Martinique, Montserrat, and Nevis
Inaugural Bledisloe Cup rugby match played between Australia and New Zealand at Eden Park, Auckland - NZ win 20-13
"Monkees" premieres on NBC-TV
Soviet unmanned spacecraft Luna 16 launched to the moon
US, United Kingdom, France, USSR, East & West Germanys sign agreements allowing 2 Germanys to merge

South Africa claims 3rd Tri Nations Rugby Series with 32-29 win over defending champions New Zealand in Hamilton; Steyn bros dominate with the boot; Morné kicks 2 penalties, 2 conversions & dropped goal; François, 3 penalties
Nathan Haleleaves Harlem Heights Camp (127th St) for spy mission
Gustav Mahler's 8th Symphony "Symphony of a Thousand" premieres in Munich with 1,028 musicians
Stefan EdbergbeatsMichael Chang6-7, 7-5, 7-6, 5-7, 6-4 in 5 hours and 26 minutes, the longest match in US Open history at the time (eclipsed in 2024)
Sir Ian Holm, eminent English actor (The Lord of the Rings, Alien, King Lear), born in Goodmayes, Essex (d. 2020)
Leslie Cheung, Hong Kong actor, singer and composer (Farewell My Concubine), born in Hong Kong (d. 2003)
Silke Hörner, German breaststroke swimmer (2 Olympic gold 1988), born in Leipzig, Germany
Larry LaLonde, American rock guitarist (Primus - "Frizzle Fry"), born in Richmond, California
Darren Campbell, British athlete (Olympic gold 4x100m relay 2004, silver 200m 2000), born in Manchester, England
Yao Ming, Chinese basketball player, born in Shanghai
Elina Svitolina, Ukrainian tennis player (WTA Finals champion 2018), born in Odessa, Ukraine
Canyonlands National Park is designated as a National Park.
World's first patent for synthetic rubber granted to German chemist Fritz Hofmann
United States' first known female police officer, Alice Stebbins Wells, is appointed by the LAPD
WHYY TV channel 12 in Wilmington, DE (PBS) begins broadcasting
Supersonic airliner Concorde lands for the 1st time at Heathrow airport
European Championship marathon won by Dutchman Gerard Nijboer (2:15:16) &Rosa Motaof Portugal (2:36:04)
Desmond Llewelyn, Welsh actor (James Bond films, 1963-99), WWII army veteran and POW, born in Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales (d. 1999)
